text:
Arrival × At × The Arena (トウギジョウ×ニ×トウジョウ, Tōgijō × Ni × Tōjō) is the 27 th episode of the Hunter × Hunter 2011 series. It first aired on April 15 th , 2012.

Overview

Having finally arrived at Heavens Arena, Gon and Killua begin to quickly rise up the floors of the arena. During that time, they meet a young fighter named Zushi along with his master, Wing, and during Killua's fight with Zushi, after sensing a mysterious aura from Zushi, Gon and Killua are introduced to Nen.

Summary

Traveling by airship, Gon spots the enormous tower, and Killua reveals that it's called Heavens Arena, adding that it has 251 floors and is 991 meters tall (the fourth tallest building in the world). He tells Gon that he used up all his money on the trip fare and that they will need to make more inside the tower. The two plan to register for the tournament to earn money while training at the same time. Gon is surprised by the long line, and Killua explains that unlike the Hunter Exam, Heavens Arena doesn't have any conditions to participate besides defeating one's opponent.

After registering, the two enter a crowded arena and wait until their numbers are called. Killua says that it's been a while and that the place hasn't changed. Gon asks him if he's been there before, and Killua replies that he competed when he was only six after his father left him there with no money and told him to reach the 200th floor before returning home. He reveals that it took him two years to do so, and Gon is surprised that it took him so long even though he was only six. Killua then tells Gon that if he wants to fight opponents of Hisoka's level, he will need to reach the higher floors of the tower.

Gon's number is called, and he nervously prepares to fight, with Killua assuring him that it will be easy for him since he was able to open the Testing Gate. Gon enters the ring and looks across at his enormous opponent as the referee explains the rules and begins the match. The large man promises to end things with a single punch, but Gon easily dodges the attack and uses a simple palm strike just as Killua instructed him, sending the man flying into the wall behind him. As Gon looks at his hand and wonders when he became so strong, the referee gives him a ticket and tells him that he can make his way to the 50th floor. After easily defeating his opponent as well, Killua is told that he can go to the 180th floor since he has reached the 200th floor before, but he politely declines and decides to start at the 50th floor with Gon. Beside Killua, another young boy easily wins his match and also prepares to make his way to the 50th floor.

As the three make their way to the 50th floor, an elevator attendant explains how moving up the tower works, and the boy adds that he has heard they will get their own room if they make it to the 100th floor, introducing himself as Zushi. Gon and Killua also gives their names as they reach the 50th floor. Zushi tells Killua how impressed he was by his match, but Killua points out that he made it there in one fight as well. Zushi humbly replies that he has a long way to go, asking the two which discipline of martial arts they use and revealing that he uses the Shingen-ryu fighting technique. The two reply that they don't belong to any school, and Zushi can't believe how strong they are with no training.

Zushi's master suddenly appears and praises his student for following his teachings. After Zushi introduces Gon and Killua, Zushi's master introduces himself as Wing and tells them that he didn't expect to see other children, wondering why they are there. Killua tells him that they hope to get stronger and make money. Wing realizes that while they must be skilled to reach the 50th floor, they should pay close attention to their opponents. The three boys then head to a nearby kiosk and receive their prize money of 152. Killua immediately buys a canned drink with the money and reveals that a win in the 50s has a reward of 50,000. He then adds that wins in the 100s are worth 1 million, leaving Gon and Zushi stunned. Killua continues by saying that any wins past the 150th floor are worth 10 million each, with Gon remembering that Killua made it to the 200th floor when he was younger and wondering how much he made. Killua replies that while he quit once he reached it, he had around 200 million in the 190s and spent it all on snacks in four years, with Gon and Zushi left speechless.

The three then make their way to a crowded waiting room to prepare for their next match, with Killua assuring them that they will easily win. It's suddenly announced that Killua and Zushi will fight each other on the 57th floor. Killua bluntly tells Gon that he will wait for him above, already confident that he will easily win the match. The announcer introduces the two fighters and shows highlights from their previous matches, asking the crowd to place their bets on who will win. The odds are in Zushi's favor, and Killua is left frustrated by the result. The referee then explains the rules and begins the match. Although impressed by Zushi's fighting stance, Killua calmly walks toward him and avoids his punch before swiftly striking the back of his neck. Killua earns two points for the strike as the referee checks on Zushi, who suddenly rises to his feet and leaves Killua surprised. Zushi unleashes a barrage of punches, but Killua easily dodges them all and again strikes the back of his neck. Zushi recovers again and decides to resort to a new stance that leaves Killua terrified and causes him to quickly leap back. Watching among the crowd, Wing suddenly screams Zushi's name and causes the spectators around him to put their hands over their ears. He sits down with a stern expression on his face, and Zushi appears visibly shaken before the announcer restarts the match.

Killua eventually meets up with Gon, who reveals that he won 60,000 before wondering what took him so long. Killua tells him that while Zushi wasn't strong, he has potential and will keep improving. He then reveals that while he was able to knock him around with ease, he couldn't defeat him, admitting that he had to win by TKO from points. He then reveals that Zushi's aura during his new stance reminded him of his brother's, oblivious to what it was but confident that it's dangerous. He guesses that it's a technique and tells Gon that Wing called it Ren. In a flashback, Killua sees Zushi on his hands and knees apologizing to Wing, who reminds him that he wasn't supposed to use Ren yet. Zushi says that he couldn't resist against such a strong opponent, and Wing tells him that while wanting to win is good, he needs to remember his true goal of reaching the top floor. He then adds that focusing on a single win will reduce his potential and that he needs to be willing to lose a thousand times.

Back in the present, Gon wonders if Zushi isn't supposed to use Ren until reaching the top floor, and Killua reveals that he now plans to reach the top floor as well, with Gon happily agreeing and telling himself that it was his original goal anyway. Gon and Killua continue to easily defeat all their opponents over the next few days, with both reaching the 100s. They each receive a private room, and while Gon is happy that they don't have to worry about finding a place, Killua points out that if they drop below the 100s, they have to check out. He then warns Gon that the competition will become more difficult since every opponent wants to keep their spot. However, the two continue to easily win and receive more prize money, with both noting that Zushi is still in the 50s. Gon again wonders about the technique called Ren, and while Killua thinks about encountering it with opponents on the higher floors, Gon suggests simply asking Zushi about it.

The two do just that, and Zushi explains that it's one of the four major principles for training one's mind and body. He adds that one must know Ten, learn Zetsu, achieve Ren, and reach Hatsu, which are all part of Nen training. As Killua angrily points out that he didn't understand any of what he said, Wing suddenly appears and wonders if Zushi should be teaching them. Killua tells him that he is eager to know what it is since it's connected to the secret of his brother's power. Wing asks if his brother is able to use Nen, but Killua points out that he doesn't even know what Nen and Ren mean, agreeing to go through the complete learning process. Wing tells the two to join him and Zushi at his house as they prepare to learn about the secret technique of Nen.
